methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Addionics is a Series B climate and energy technology company that has raised $39M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine comparable companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private—across the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $39M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $74M. The peer-median method identifies six directly comparable same-sector, same-stage companies with a median valuation of $355M and a range spanning $120M to $5.0B, reflecting meaningful dispersion within the cohort.

The triangulated analysis across both methods produces a valuation range of $63M to $408M, with a median estimate of $215M. The wide range reflects two key uncertainties: the substantial variance within the peer set (a five-fold spread between median and maximum) and the limited signal from stage-progression relative to market comparables. The peer-median method carries greater weight given the direct comparability of the six companies, though the presence of outliers—particularly Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B—underscores sector-wide heterogeneity in risk and technology readiness.
